title: quercus tonduzii
intro: Quercus tonduzii, also known as the Central American white oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Central America. The medicinal properties of Quercus tonduzii lie in its leaves, bark, and roots, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus tonduzii include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and promote digestive health. Herbal preparations of Quercus tonduzii often involve infusions, decoctions, and tinctures made from its leaves, bark, and roots, which can be consumed as teas, taken as capsules, or applied topically to the skin.
